entries.definition:
the reference in verses 1–16 to the story of the Prophet’s ‘Frowning’ at the blind man.
عبوس cabūs [quasi-act. part.] stern, dismal, frowning (76:10) إنا نخاف من ربنا يوما عبوسا قمطريرا we fear from our Lord a frowning grim Day.
entries.definition_english:
the reference in verses 1–16 to the story of the Prophet’s ‘Frowning’ at the blind man.
عبوس cabūs [quasi-act. part.] stern, dismal, frowning (76:10) إنا نخاف من ربنا يوما عبوسا قمطريرا we fear from our Lord a frowning grim Day.

entries.definition:
c-b-q-r this root is said to originate from the word عبقر cabqar, the name of the place which the ancient Arabs believed to be the home of the wondrous jinn. It is said also to be a name of a town, either in the Yemen or in ʾal-Jazīratu, where cloth of great beauty is made. Some scholars, however, suggest that the word عبقر came into Arabic from Persian long before the revelation of the Qurʾan. The concepts associated with this root include genius, talent, poetical inspiration and rich floral types of carpet (revealing perhaps a Persian origin for the word). Of this root, one form, عبقري cabqariyy, occurs in a single place in the Qurʾan.
عبقري cabqariyy [n; nisbatun to عبقر cabqar] richly designed carpet (55:76) متكئين على رفرف خضر وعبقري حسان they will be reclining on green cushions and fine carpets.
entries.definition_english:

Some scholars, however, suggest that the word عبقر came into Arabic from Persian long before the revelation of the Qurʾan. The concepts associated with this root include genius, talent, poetical inspiration and rich floral types of carpet (revealing perhaps a Persian origin for the word). Of this root, one form, عبقري cabqariyy, occurs in a single place in the Qurʾan.
عبقري cabqariyy [n; nisbatun to عبقر cabqar] richly designed carpet (55:76) متكئين على رفرف خضر وعبقري حسان they will be reclining on green cushions and fine carpets.
